title: Transactions, Isolation, Concurrency, and Recovery · Data-System Transfer
summary: Two operators reassign and resolve the same ticket while a failure occurs between related writes. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a concurrency-safe ticket transition protocol with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability using a multi-statement business invariant.
Bound a unit of work with BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and savepoints and verify failure behavior.
Recognize dirty read, nonrepeatable read, phantom, lost update, and write-skew schedules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Acid Transactions
steps.instruction:
lab case — Operators A and B read ticket 103 as open; A assigns it while B resolves it and a failure occurs between event insert and status update. Identify the anomaly, transaction boundary, retry precondition, and restore evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Acid Transactions.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability using a multi-statement business invariant. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Acid Transactions, keep Acid Transactions connected to executable relational evidence.
